stories.title: Little Pea Shop Padded Crib Bumpers — CPSC Formal Recall
stories.angle: Federal law has banned padded crib bumpers since 2022. Little Pea Shop kept selling them anyway. Here's what to do if you have them.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: 🚨 RECALL: Little Pea Shop padded crib bumpers. Sold littlepeashop.com Oct 2023–Oct 2025, $30–$80. Violates federal safe sleep ban — suffocation risk. Stop use now. Full refund at cpsc.gov #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Padded crib bumpers have been federally banned since 2022. The safest crib has one thing: a bare mattress with a tight-fitting sheet. If you have padded bumpers from any brand, stop using them. Check cpsc.gov #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
🚨 If you have padded crib bumpers from Little Pea Shop, stop using them now.

The CPSC has issued a formal recall for Little Pea Shop padded crib bumpers. They were sold on littlepeashop.com between October 2023 and October 2025, priced between $30 and $80. The problem: padded crib bumpers are federally banned under the Safe Sleep for Babies Act, which became law in May 2022. They can obstruct an infant's breathing and have been linked to suffocation deaths.

The recall means you're entitled to a full refund. Stop using the bumpers immediately and contact Little Pea Shop — or visit cpsc.gov for step-by-step instructions on the return process.

It's worth knowing that the only safe sleeping surface for an infant is a bare crib mattress with a tight-fitting sheet. No bumpers, no loose blankets, no pillows — regardless of how cozy they look. If you have padded bumpers from any brand, not just Little Pea Shop, this is a good moment to remove them.

Have a question about safe sleep setups? Drop it in the comments. 👇
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
🚨 CPSC RECALL: Little Pea Shop padded crib bumpers. Sold Oct 2023–Oct 2025, $30–$80. Federal ban violation — can block an infant's breathing. Stop use. Full refund available at cpsc.gov. Safe sleep = bare mattress + tight-fitting sheet. Nothing else.
```

article.headline: CPSC Recalls Little Pea Shop Padded Crib Bumpers for Violating Federal Safe Sleep Ban

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Little Pea Shop Crib Bumpers — CPSC Recall

### Image 1A — X/Twitter (1200×675)

**Visual prompt:**
A close-up photograph of a white wooden baby crib interior, completely bare — just a firm white mattress with a clean white fitted sheet, no bumpers, no pillows, no stuffed animals. Soft morning window light illuminates the crib from the left. The overall mood is calm, clean, and safe. Warm neutral tones. Photorealistic style. Leave the bottom third of the image with soft, uncluttered light for text overlay.

### Image 1B — Facebook/Article Hero (1200×630)

**Visual prompt:**
A flat-lay overhead view of a clean, empty baby crib mattress with a white fitted sheet. Next to the crib, a single folded crib bumper set sits off to the side — clearly set aside and unused. Soft warm light, clean background. The mood is informative, not alarming. Photorealistic style. Bottom third clear for text overlay.

---

research.facts:
Little Pea Shop Padded Crib Bumpers — CPSC Formal Recall
**Confidence: HIGH**
**Source:** cpsc.gov
- Little Pea Shop padded crib bumpers formally recalled by CPSC
- Sold on littlepeashop.com from October 2023 through October 2025
- Price range: $30–$80
- Hazard: padded crib bumpers can obstruct an infant's breathing, posing a suffocation risk
- Violation: Safe Sleep for Babies Act (signed into law May 2022), which bans the manufacture and sale of padded crib bumpers in the United States
- Remedy: consumers should stop using the bumpers immediately and contact Little Pea Shop for a full refund
- No injuries reported (standard recall language)
- Formal recall (not a warning) — refund available
- cpsc.gov: "Little Pea Shop Crib Bumpers Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Suffocation; Violates Federal Crib Bumper Ban; Sold by Little Pea Shop"
**Background context (from CPSC and AAP):**
- Padded crib bumpers have been linked to infant suffocation deaths since the 1990s
- The Safe Sleep for Babies Act (SSBA), signed May 16, 2022, banned the manufacture and sale of padded crib bumpers — and inclined infant sleep products — in the U.S.
- AAP has long advised: bare crib with a tight-fitting sheet is the only safe sleep surface for infants
- Despite the federal ban, some sellers continued marketing padded bumpers online; CPSC has issued multiple recalls and warnings in 2026 for violations

stories.title: El Paso County Fair — Opens This Weekend (July 11–18, Calhan)
stories.angle: The El Paso County Fair opens tomorrow — your opening weekend game plan for families in Colorado Springs.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: El Paso County Fair opens tomorrow 🎡 July 11–18 in Calhan, ~30 min east on Hwy 24. $1 general admission. Rides, CPRA rodeo, demolition derby, live music. Fri–Sat open until 11 PM. elpasocountyfair.com #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: El Paso County Fair has a Sensory Day for kids with sensory sensitivities — a sensory-friendly space + Sensory Book from the Autism Society of CO. Fair runs July 11–18 in Calhan. $1 admission. elpasocountyfair.com #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
The El Paso County Fair opens tomorrow 🎡 — and it's one of the best family deals within an hour of Colorado Springs.

The 120th annual fair runs July 11–18 at the El Paso County Fairgrounds in Calhan, about 30 minutes east of Colorado Springs on Hwy 24. General admission is $1. If you want to go multiple days or catch the evening events (rodeo, demolition derby), the $69 Golden Ticket covers it all. First responders, educators, and healthcare workers get $2 off with their ID.

Here's what's there: carnival rides, a CPRA rodeo, a demolition derby, live music, games, and cornhole. Hours run Friday and Saturday 10 AM–11 PM, Sunday 10 AM–9 PM, and Monday through Thursday 11 AM–9 PM.

One thing that doesn't get enough attention: the fair has a Sensory Day that includes a sensory-friendly space and a Sensory Book from the Autism Society of Colorado to help families navigate the fair. It makes the whole experience more accessible for kids who can get overwhelmed by crowds and noise.

Are you heading to the fair this weekend? 🌵
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
🎡 El Paso County Fair opens TOMORROW — July 11–18 in Calhan (~30 min east of Colorado Springs on Hwy 24). $1 general admission. Rides, CPRA rodeo, demolition derby, live music. Fri–Sat 10 AM–11 PM, Sun 10 AM–9 PM, Mon–Thu 11 AM–9 PM. Sensory Day available. elpasocountyfair.com
```

stories.title: Babypark Children's Carriers — CPSC Warning, Deadly Fall Hazard
stories.angle: If you have a Babypark soft carrier, stop using it now. The CPSC says the leg openings are large enough for an infant to slip through — a deadly fall risk.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: ⚠️ CPSC Warning: Babypark Children's Carriers. Leg openings are large enough for an infant to slip through — a deadly fall hazard. Violates mandatory standard for soft infant carriers. Stop use immediately. cpsc.gov #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Soft carrier check: leg openings should fit snugly around your child's thighs — not loosely. If legs slide through easily, the carrier isn't safe. The Babypark warning is a reminder to check yours. cpsc.gov #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
⚠️ If you own a Babypark soft baby carrier, the CPSC is asking you to stop using it immediately.

The warning covers Babypark Children's Carriers. The leg openings are large enough for an infant to slip through — which means your child could fall out. That's not a minor fit issue. It's a potentially fatal fall hazard, and it violates the mandatory federal standard for soft infant and toddler carriers.

Unlike a formal recall, a CPSC Warning doesn't always come with a built-in refund program. The guidance right now is to stop using the carrier and check cpsc.gov for the latest updates on next steps. If you purchased through a retailer, it's worth reaching out directly about a refund.

While you're at it, this is a good moment to check any soft carrier you use. The leg openings should fit snugly around your child's thighs. If your child's legs slide easily through the openings or there's a lot of extra fabric, that's worth looking into. Carriers should also be checked regularly for worn buckles, frayed seams, and stretched straps.

Have you ever had a carrier fit issue or safety concern? What brand do you use? 👇
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
⚠️ CPSC Warning: Babypark Children's Carriers. Leg openings large enough for an infant to slip through — deadly fall hazard. Stop use immediately. Violates mandatory standard for soft infant carriers. For updates, visit cpsc.gov. Check all carriers for snug leg opening fit and buckle integrity.
```

article.headline: CPSC Warning: Stop Using Babypark Children's Carriers — Infant Can Slip Through Leg Openings

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Babypark Children's Carriers — CPSC Warning

### Image 3A — X/Twitter (1200×675)

**Visual prompt:**
A close-up of a soft infant carrier being put on by a parent — focus on the leg openings where the infant's legs should rest, with emphasis on proper fit. Neutral background. Clean, educational, calm tone — not alarming. Soft diffused light. Photorealistic style. Bottom third clear for text overlay.

### Image 3B — Facebook/Article Hero (1200×630)

**Visual prompt:**
A parent carrying a baby in a soft front-facing baby carrier, standing in a bright room. Baby is visible from behind with legs in proper M-position — knees higher than hips. Clean, light background. Safe and caring mood. Photorealistic. No brand logos. Bottom third clear for text overlay.

---

research.facts:
Babypark Children's Carriers — CPSC Warning
**Confidence: HIGH**
**Source:** cpsc.gov
- CPSC warning (not a formal recall) for Babypark Children's Carriers
- Hazard: leg openings are large enough that a child can slip through, posing a deadly fall hazard
- Violation: mandatory standard for soft infant and toddler carriers
- Action required: stop using immediately
- Remedy: as a CPSC Warning (not recall), there may not be a direct refund program; consumers should visit cpsc.gov for the latest guidance and contact the seller directly
- No specific unit count found (typical for CPSC Warnings vs. formal recalls)
**Carrier safety background (from AAP and safe-use guidelines):**
- Soft infant carriers must keep babies in a proper "M" position with hips flexed above the knees
- Leg openings that are too large allow infants to slip through feet-first — a known fall risk
- Mandatory federal standard for soft infant/toddler carriers: 16 CFR Part 1231 (effective March 2022)
- The standard covers structural integrity, buckle strength, leg opening dimensions, and weight limits
- Parents should regularly check carrier seams, buckles, and leg openings for wear or excess slack

stories.title: Free COS Summer Parks + Outdoor Activities Guide
stories.angle: A practical guide to free outdoor summer activities for Colorado Springs families — no tickets, no registration, no crowds indoors.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Free COS family outdoor spots 🌄 Garden of the Gods (always free), North Cheyenne Cañon (Helen Hunt Falls, easy 0.4 mi), Palmer Park. Go early — storms arrive 2–4 PM in July. Bring extra water at altitude. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Some of the best things to do in Colorado Springs this summer are completely free 🌄 — and they're right outside your door.

Garden of the Gods is always free. The red rock formations make it one of the most photographed spots in the state, and the trails range from paved and fully accessible to longer loop hikes. There's also a free Visitor Center worth a stop, especially with curious kids. North Cheyenne Cañon Park is also free, and the Helen Hunt Falls hike is one of the best easy family hikes in the area — just 0.4 miles round trip with a waterfall at the end. Palmer Park has trails for all levels and is a great dog-friendly option in the northeast part of the city.

A few altitude tips for July: go earlier in the day if you can. Afternoon thunderstorms typically roll in between 2 and 4 PM this time of year, and they can come on fast. Colorado Springs sits at about 6,035 feet, so pack more water than you think you'll need — the altitude and dry air dehydrate you faster than you'd expect. SPF is non-negotiable up here too.

Which of these spots is your family's go-to outdoor space this summer? 🏔️
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
🌄 Free summer parks in Colorado Springs: Garden of the Gods (always free, paved + unpaved trails), North Cheyenne Cañon Park (Helen Hunt Falls — easy 0.4 mi round trip), Palmer Park (trails + picnic areas). Go before noon to beat afternoon storms. Bring extra water — 6,035 ft elevation. coloradosprings.gov
```

research.facts:
Free COS Summer Parks + Outdoor Activities Guide
**Confidence: HIGH**
**Source:** coloradosprings.gov, gardenofgods.com, local parks info, NOAA climate data
- Garden of the Gods: always free; Red Rock Canyon Road access; Visitor Center (free); iconic red rock formations; easy to moderate trails; paved and unpaved; wheelchair accessible sections; gardenofgods.com
- North Cheyenne Cañon Park: free; open daily; Helen Hunt Falls hike is a family favorite (0.4 mile round trip easy trail to falls); North Cheyenne Cañon Road; Silver Cascade Falls also accessible; coloradosprings.gov
- Palmer Park: free; large open park with trails and picnic areas; off Maizeland Road in northeast COS; dog-friendly
- Average July high in Colorado Springs: approximately 83°F at elevation 6,035 ft
- Afternoon thunderstorms are common, typically 2–4 PM in July
- Altitude considerations: Colorado Springs sits at 6,035 ft; visitors and residents alike need extra hydration; UV exposure is stronger at altitude
- Recommended hiking tips: go early (before noon), bring 16–24 oz water per child per hour, apply SPF 30+ sunscreen, watch weather
- coloradosprings.gov/parks

stories.title: Bancroft Park Tuesday Night Concerts — Free Family Music All Summer
stories.angle: Free live music in downtown Colorado Springs every Tuesday night this summer. Here's what to know about Bancroft Park concerts.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Free live music every Tuesday at Bancroft Park in Colorado Springs — 6–8 PM, June through October. Bring a blanket. All ages, no tickets needed. A great free weeknight option this summer. coloradosprings.gov #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Free live music every Tuesday night this summer — right in Colorado Springs 🎶

Bancroft Park in Old Colorado City hosts free outdoor concerts every Tuesday, 6–8 PM, from June through October. No tickets, no registration, no cover charge. Bring a blanket, lawn chairs, and a picnic if you want — or just show up and enjoy it.

It's one of those easy summer weeknight plans that doesn't require much coordination. The kids can run around while you actually get to listen to live music, which is more than you can say for most Tuesday evenings. Parking is available near the Old Colorado City shops, and the neighborhood is walkable and family-friendly.

Check coloradosprings.gov for current lineup information and any schedule updates.

What's your favorite free outdoor event in Colorado Springs this summer? 👇
```
facebook.image_caption:
```
🎶 Free Tuesday night concerts at Bancroft Park in Colorado Springs — 6–8 PM, June through October. Bring blankets and lawn chairs. All ages, no tickets. Old Colorado City neighborhood. A great free weeknight option for families all summer long. coloradosprings.gov
```
